propertyType.description: Google Analytics 4 is the latest version. Universal Analytics is an older version that is not fully functional after the end of June 2023.

metricsGA4.description: The quantitative measurements of a report. For example, the metric eventCount is the total number of events. Requests are allowed up to 10 metrics.

expression.description: A mathematical expression for derived metrics. For example, the metric Event count per user is eventCount/totalUsers.

invisible.description: Whether a metric is invisible in the report response. If a metric is invisible, the metric will not produce a column in the response, but can be used in metricFilter, orderBys, or a metric expression.

expression.description: A mathematical expression for derived metrics. For example, the metric Event count per user is eventCount/totalUsers.

invisible.description: Whether a metric is invisible in the report response. If a metric is invisible, the metric will not produce a column in the response, but can be used in metricFilter, orderBys, or a metric expression.

dimensionsGA4.description: Dimensions are attributes of your data. For example, the dimension city indicates the city from which an event originates. Dimension values in report responses are strings; for example, the city could be "Paris" or "New York". Requests are allowed up to 9 dimensions.

name.description: The name of the dimension. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.
The name of the dimension. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

simple.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

currencyCode.description: A currency code in ISO4217 format, such as "AED", "USD", "JPY". If the field is empty, the report uses the property's default currency.

keepEmptyRows.description: Whether false or unspecified, each row with all metrics equal to 0 will not be returned. If true, these rows will be returned if they are not separately removed by a filter.

orderByUI.description: Specifies how rows are ordered in the response

desc.description: Whether true, sorts by descending order

metricName.description: Sorts by metric values. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

desc.description: Whether true, sorts by descending order

dimensionName.description: Sorts by metric values. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

ALPHANUMERIC.description: Alphanumeric sort by Unicode code point

CASE_INSENSITIVE_ALPHANUMERIC.description: Case insensitive alphanumeric sort by lower case Unicode code point

NUMERIC.description: Dimension values are converted to numbers before sorting

returnPropertyQuota.description: Whether to return the current state of this Analytics Property's quota. Quota is returned in PropertyQuota.

name.description: Name of the dimension to fetch, for example ga:browser. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

operator.description: Operator to use in combination with value

expressions.description: String or regular expression to match against

hideTotals.description: Whether to hide the total of all metrics for all the matching rows, for every date range

hideValueRanges.description: Whether to hide the minimum and maximum across all matching rows

includeEmptyRows.description: Whether the response exclude rows if all the retrieved metrics are equal to zero

useResourceQuotas.description: Whether to enable resource based quotas

viewId.description: The view from Google Analytics. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.
